meaning: v.娴告病,娣规病,鎺╂病vi.娼滄按
examples: The waters were rising about the rock and would soon submerge it.
Their storage reservoirs stop the flow of rivers and often submerge towns, farms, and historic sites.
The submarine had had time to submerge before the warship could approach.
At the most, a weir is constructed to submerge the mouth of the penstock.
If loose lips sink ships, a too-revealing mystery shopper could certainly submerge an hotelier or two.
